title: randia horrida
intro: Randia horrida, also known as the "wild jasmine", is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Randia horrida include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Randia horrida is typically made into tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
